author: @LEOJNAMOR
text:
I appreciate this video. This is the second video that I have seen from this channel, and it hits hard, because I wasn’t aware I was doing something similar to my colleagues, friends, relationships and family. I always thought I was innocently expressing my passion and love for learning new and exciting things. I wasn’t aware it would offend anyone, in fact, I know that feeling very well. I WAS a poor student, I wasn’t rowdy, I didn’t disturb the class session. I simply struggled mentally to understand things due to both the social dynamics in school and physical/emotional abuse at home. Some of the teachers, not all, didn’t understand how it felt to be the student they used as an analogy for stupidity, (I doubt they regretted doing so). However I despise them for it, I felt great shame, till I eventually started to believe the words they would say. It didn’t help that my social circle at home was similar, these words, their words, it all etched itself onto me, poisoning me emotionally for a long time. I remembered though, the day when I made a promise in the form of a wish, from myself to the world, God, or whatever energy that cared to listened. I was eleven years old. A promise to learn everything the world had to offer, to constantly grow and never stop growing, to move away from the contumelious environment that I viewed as a prison cell, to travel the world, to learn the world’s history, experience different culture, meet people, learn to speak different languages; etc. To be surrounded by true friends, a family to call my own, to fall in love and be love in returned. Continue caring uncondionally for friends, family and strangers alike, to be generous, respectful and kind. To live honorably, this was all my promise, my wish, my creed, my willpower, it has become my mantra. 


I’m sorry if this rant on, or doesn’t connect with the message of the video, but it’s how I genuinely felt after watching it. Long story short, I feel that if someone could understand what I experienced and how I persevered through it all, then they could see I don’t mean any harm, nor disrespect, I’m simply keeping up the promise I made to my eleven years ago old self. I love to learn, teach, and what better way than to grow with people.

Stay Blessed.

author: @AlisonFennell
text: Really lovely to hear that - I felt my shoulders drop as this truth sunk in - we are always striving and pushing when really we are enough. Great - thanks - Alison

author: @paulnagle5792
text: I was a bit worried at first that Robert's solo performance was being corrupted but you sound very competent, compassionate and well meaning.  2 things I think you all need to do ASAP is automated testing use arduinos, PC or pi etc and sensors to data log and conduct experiments and graph results using standard methods and publish them. I think you should do at least 10-11 experiments simultaneously ie. different concentrations of dielectric etc. Engage the public more and use the power of good will.. Endlessly renewable if used nicely and it's free.

replies.author: @AshleyMillsTube
replies.text: Paul Nagle cheers mate, love the ideas around automation, i'm a bit fascinated with automation actually, hope to get the chance to bring some of this on board

author: @VAXKILLEDMYDAD
text: Definitely worth reflecting on these thoughts mate, especially for teachers. I often forget that not only am I interested in grammar, but also I teach it every day, and so what I see as obvious will NOT be obvious for others with a different profession. Difficult things take time to learn, and I appreciate this video for reminding me that we should always be patient and remember that others have spent their time learning different skills.

author: @chaitanyabhave2092
text: Robert Murray-Smith  what is your experience as a researcher? I am 19 and love doing experimentation work(much to the chargrin of my parents-work gets messy). I have previously worked with electronics, and a little dabbling in materials science and chemistry. I plan on doing research as a career, but my parents opine that I should do something more lucrative. Personally not doing research feels like a depressing concept to me. What would you suggest I do?

text: wow, thank you.  this brings a lot of peace to my world today.  Thank you.

author: @stevesimpson5994
text:
One of the crying shames in this world endless "self-betterment", is that we've forgotten how to deal with our individual imperfections.

To be human is to seek from others the things we lack, and to supply to others what we have in abundance.

For instance, I am terrible at organising things (because I find it draining / boring), but I know others excel at and enjoy organisation.  So, I actively seek out organisers and am willing to "be organised" even if I don't like it, because I have experienced the benefits of it.

In the case of say, prime number theory, you have expertise.  It's true many people would be bored to tears by the theory, but they may "warm up" to its applications (eg. cryptography).  To these people, the most useful communication would be, "Value the math-heads, because they help you to do things you couldn't do by yourself."

Don't stress too much :-)
